title: Ito tests pull requests by running the app, not just reading code
friendlyTitle: Ito checks whether code changes actually work
summary: Ito is positioning itself as an agentic QA platform that deploys an application in an isolated browser environment and tests pull requests for behavioral regressions, broken flows, UI issues, and runtime failures. The product reports findings with screenshots, video, and PR-level QA details.
friendlySummary: Ito runs an app in a browser and tests pull requests for broken flows, UI problems, and bugs. It gives teams reports with screenshots and videos.
whyItMatters: AI code review is starting to move from static suggestions into execution-based verification. If these systems work well, teams can catch user-facing bugs earlier and reserve human review time for product judgment and edge cases.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help developers find problems before users do, instead of only relying on code comments or manual testing.
